headline: SIXTH STRAIGHT DEFICIT: Silver Institute's 2026 World Survey Forecasts 67M-oz Shortfall as Investment Demand Holds
snippet: The Silver Institute released its World Silver Survey 2026 on April 15, projecting a sixth consecutive annual market deficit of 67 million ounces. Silver investment demand is forecast to remain robust even as industrial fabrication moderates, with the report characterizing 2026 as a maturing bull market with structural deficits showing no signs of abating.

headline: 215 g/t GOLD OVER 5.35m: West Red Lake Hits Bonanza Grade at Ontario's Madsen Mine 904 Complex
snippet: West Red Lake Gold Mines (TSX-V: WRLG) reported a 215.46 g/t gold intercept over 5.35 metres at the Austin 904 Complex within its Madsen Mine in the Red Lake Gold District, Ontario. Two additional holes returned 50.34 g/t over 4.05m and 61.70 g/t over 3m, confirming high-grade continuity at depth in a zone targeted as the primary drilling focus through the rest of 2026.

headline: EXPANDING STRIKE: Trident Drills 4.61 g/t Au over 38m at Contact Lake — New Hole 200m Beyond 2025 Discovery
snippet: Trident Resources Corp. intersected 4.61 g/t Au over 38.48 metres from 77m at the Contact Lake Gold Project in northern Saskatchewan, including 5.48 g/t over 26.14m in the higher-grade core. A second hole cut 11.97 g/t over 10.70m at 324 metres depth — 200 metres northeast of 2025 drilling — demonstrating the deposit is extending simultaneously along strike and at depth.

headline: CADIA HALTED: Newmont Suspends Australia Mine After 4.5 Earthquake — 153 Workers Evacuated, No Return Timeline
snippet: Newmont Corporation suspended underground operations at its Cadia gold-copper mine in New South Wales on April 14 after a 4.5-magnitude earthquake; all 153 workers underground were safely evacuated with no injuries. NEM shares fell 4.63% on the news — Cadia produced 385,000 oz gold and 82,000 tonnes of copper in 2025, and a 2017 quake on the identical date kept the mine shut for three months.

headline: GOLD HITS $4,820: Precious Metals Rally as Iran Ceasefire Signals Emerge and Dollar Falls to Six-Week Low
snippet: Gold climbed to $4,820 per ounce on April 15 — up approximately 2% on the day — as hopes of a US-Iran ceasefire extension gave fresh impetus to precious metals and the dollar slipped to a six-week low. Silver rose in tandem approaching $80 per ounce, with a tentative two-week truce reported to be under discussion for extension by two further weeks.

headline: COPPER ERASES WAR LOSSES: LME Metal Hits One-Month High as Iran Peace Talks Revive Industrial Demand Optimism
snippet: Copper rose to its highest level in over a month on April 14-15, surpassing the pre-conflict closing level of $13,343 per tonne as optimism mounted over potential US-Iran peace negotiations. The recovery marks a sharp reversal from wartime selling that had dragged the metal below levels justified by its structural supply-deficit fundamentals.

headline: RARE EARTH SHOCK: China Northern Hikes Q2 Prices 44% — Stocks Jump as Supply Chain Alarms Sound
snippet: China Northern Rare Earth Group set its Q2 2026 concentrate price at 38,804 yuan per tonne — a 44.6% jump from Q1 and more than double year-ago levels — sending rare earth producer stocks sharply higher while raising concerns about demand destruction in EV, defence, and clean energy supply chains. The move follows China's earlier block on sulphuric acid exports critical to rare earth ore processing, further tightening the Western supply picture.

headline: SWISS BANK RESETS TARGET: UBP Rebuilds Gold to 6% of Portfolios, Holds Firm on $6,000 Year-End Call
snippet: Union Bancaire Privée — managing $233 billion in client assets — reaffirmed its $6,000/oz year-end gold target on April 13, rebuilding discretionary client exposure from 3% back to roughly 6% after trimming during the Iran-war pullback. Deutsche Bank and Societe Generale share the $6,000 target, with SocGen warning the forecast may prove conservative if fiscal deterioration accelerates.

headline: GOLD AS CANARY: April's $4,700+ Surge Is Not a Safe-Haven Trade — It's a Fiscal Stability Warning
snippet: This April 13 analysis argues gold's historic flight above $4,700 reflects something deeper than geopolitical hedging — it signals growing institutional anxiety about sovereign debt trajectories, central bank independence, and long-term dollar credibility. The piece examines why this cycle differs structurally from 2020 and what that distinction means for miners versus bullion holders.

headline: COPPER PLAYBOOK: The Pure-Play Mining ETF Built for This Tariff-Fuelled Rally Above $13,000/t
snippet: 24/7 Wall St. identifies the copper mining ETF delivering the most concentrated leverage to the metal's record-breaking run, driven by Trump's Section 232 tariff regime and AI data-center copper demand projected at 475,000 tonnes in 2026. The analysis notes that a forecast structural supply deficit of 330,000 tonnes this year separates the current cycle from prior speculative runs.

headline: ASIAN METALS WRAP: Gold Firms Near $4,800 as Ceasefire Signals Ripple Through Morning Session — April 14
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian session wrap covers gold holding firmly near $4,800 as Middle East ceasefire optimism carried through overnight trading, with silver co-moving alongside gold and base metals stabilizing after wartime volatility. The update flags the gold-to-silver ratio's ongoing contraction from its recent peak above 105 as a developing technical signal worth monitoring.
